title: petrosedum tenuifolium subsp. ibericum
intro: Petrosedum tenuifolium subsp. ibericum, also known as the Iberian houseleek, is a subspecies of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. It is a popular ornamental plant, valued for its rosettes of fleshy, silvery-green leaves and its ability to thrive in dry conditions. Petrosedum tenuifolium subsp. ibericum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, reduced fever, and protection against infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
